cards.back: A memory management strategy that divides the physical memory into fixed-size blocks called pages and uses disk storage to supplement physical memory when needed
A memory management strategy that uses disk storage to store pages of memory that are not currently in use, allowing other pages to be brought into physical memory
A memory management strategy that divides the virtual address space into fixed-size blocks called segments, each of which is allocated a fixed amount of physical memory
A memory management strategy that divides the virtual address space into isolated areas or compartments, each of which has its own set of privileges and permissions
A memory management strategy that combines physical memory with disk storage to provide a larger address space than physical memory alone, allowing multiple processes to share the same memory space
